speaker: JULIET
text: Wilt thou be gone? It is not yet near day.

speaker: JULIET
text: It was the nightingale, and not the lark,

speaker: JULIET
text: That pierced the fearful hollow of thine ear.

speaker: JULIET
text: Nightly she sings on yond pomegranate tree.

speaker: JULIET
text: Believe me, love, it was the nightingale.

speaker: ROMEO
text: It was the lark, the herald of the morn,

speaker: ROMEO
text: No nightingale. Look, love, what envious streaks

speaker: ROMEO
text: Do lace the severing clouds in yonder east.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Night’s candles are burnt out, and jocund day

speaker: ROMEO
text: Stands tiptoe on the misty mountain-tops.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I must be gone and live, or stay and die.

speaker: JULIET
text: Yond light is not daylight, I know it, I.

speaker: JULIET
text: It is some meteor that the sun exhaled

speaker: JULIET
text: To be to thee this night a torchbearer

speaker: JULIET
text: And light thee on thy way to Mantua.

speaker: JULIET
text: Therefore stay yet. Thou need’st not to be gone.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Let me be ta’en; let me be put to death.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I am content, so thou wilt have it so.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I’ll say yon gray is not the morning’s eye;

speaker: ROMEO
text: ’Tis but the pale reflex of Cynthia’s brow.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Nor that is not the lark whose notes do beat

speaker: ROMEO
text: The vaulty heaven so high above our heads.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I have more care to stay than will to go.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Come death and welcome. Juliet wills it so.

speaker: ROMEO
text: How is ’t, my soul? Let’s talk. It is not day.

speaker: JULIET
text: It is, it is. Hie hence, begone, away!

speaker: JULIET
text: It is the lark that sings so out of tune,

speaker: JULIET
text: Straining harsh discords and unpleasing sharps.

speaker: JULIET
text: Some say the lark makes sweet division.

speaker: JULIET
text: This doth not so, for she divideth us.

speaker: JULIET
text: Some say the lark and loathèd toad changed eyes.

speaker: JULIET
text: O, now I would they had changed voices too,

speaker: JULIET
text: Since arm from arm that voice doth us affray,

speaker: JULIET
text: Hunting thee hence with hunt’s-up to the day.

speaker: JULIET
text: O, now begone. More light and light it grows.

speaker: ROMEO
text: More light and light, more dark and dark our woes.

speaker: NURSE
text: Madam.

speaker: JULIET
text: Nurse?

speaker: NURSE
text: Your lady mother is coming to your chamber.

speaker: NURSE
text: The day is broke; be wary; look about.

speaker: JULIET
text: Then, window, let day in, and let life out.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Farewell, farewell. One kiss and I’ll descend.

speaker: JULIET
text: Art thou gone so? Love, lord, ay husband, friend!

speaker: JULIET
text: I must hear from thee every day in the hour,

speaker: JULIET
text: For in a minute there are many days.

speaker: JULIET
text: O, by this count I shall be much in years

speaker: JULIET
text: Ere I again behold my Romeo.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Farewell.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I will omit no opportunity

speaker: ROMEO
text: That may convey my greetings, love, to thee.

speaker: JULIET
text: O, think’st thou we shall ever meet again?

speaker: ROMEO
text: I doubt it not; and all these woes shall serve

speaker: ROMEO
text: For sweet discourses in our times to come.

speaker: JULIET
text: O God, I have an ill-divining soul!

speaker: JULIET
text: Methinks I see thee, now thou art so low,

speaker: JULIET
text: As one dead in the bottom of a tomb.

speaker: JULIET
text: Either my eyesight fails or thou lookest pale.

speaker: ROMEO
text: And trust me, love, in my eye so do you.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Dry sorrow drinks our blood. Adieu, adieu.

speaker: JULIET
text: O Fortune, Fortune, all men call thee fickle.

speaker: JULIET
text: If thou art fickle, what dost thou with him

speaker: JULIET
text: That is renowned for faith? Be fickle, Fortune,

speaker: JULIET
text: For then I hope thou wilt not keep him long,

speaker: JULIET
text: But send him back.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Ho, daughter, are you up?

speaker: JULIET
text: Who is ’t that calls? It is my lady mother.

speaker: JULIET
text: Is she not down so late or up so early?

speaker: JULIET
text: What unaccustomed cause procures her hither?

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Why, how now, Juliet?

speaker: JULIET
text: Madam, I am not well.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Evermore weeping for your cousin’s death?

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: What, wilt thou wash him from his grave with tears?

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: An if thou couldst, thou couldst not make him live.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Therefore have done. Some grief shows much of love,

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: But much of grief shows still some want of wit.

speaker: JULIET
text: Yet let me weep for such a feeling loss.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: So shall you feel the loss, but not the friend

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Which you weep for.

speaker: JULIET
text: Feeling so the loss,

speaker: JULIET
text: I cannot choose but ever weep the friend.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Well, girl, thou weep’st not so much for his death

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: As that the villain lives which slaughtered him.

speaker: JULIET
text: What villain, madam?

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: That same villain, Romeo.

speaker: JULIET
text: Villain and he be many miles asunder.—

speaker: JULIET
text: God pardon him. I do with all my heart,

speaker: JULIET
text: And yet no man like he doth grieve my heart.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: That is because the traitor murderer lives.

speaker: JULIET
text: Ay, madam, from the reach of these my hands.

speaker: JULIET
text: Would none but I might venge my cousin’s death!

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: We will have vengeance for it, fear thou not.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Then weep no more. I’ll send to one in Mantua,

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Where that same banished runagate doth live,

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Shall give him such an unaccustomed dram

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: That he shall soon keep Tybalt company.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: And then, I hope, thou wilt be satisfied.

speaker: JULIET
text: Indeed, I never shall be satisfied

speaker: JULIET
text: With Romeo till I behold him—dead—

speaker: JULIET
text: Is my poor heart, so for a kinsman vexed.

speaker: JULIET
text: Madam, if you could find out but a man

speaker: JULIET
text: To bear a poison, I would temper it,

speaker: JULIET
text: That Romeo should, upon receipt thereof,

speaker: JULIET
text: Soon sleep in quiet. O, how my heart abhors

speaker: JULIET
text: To hear him named and cannot come to him

speaker: JULIET
text: To wreak the love I bore my cousin

speaker: JULIET
text: Upon his body that hath slaughtered him.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Find thou the means, and I’ll find such a man.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: But now I’ll tell thee joyful tidings, girl.

speaker: JULIET
text: And joy comes well in such a needy time.

speaker: JULIET
text: What are they, beseech your Ladyship?

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Well, well, thou hast a careful father, child,

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: One who, to put thee from thy heaviness,

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Hath sorted out a sudden day of joy

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: That thou expects not, nor I looked not for.

speaker: JULIET
text: Madam, in happy time! What day is that?

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Marry, my child, early next Thursday morn

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: The gallant, young, and noble gentleman,

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: The County Paris, at Saint Peter’s Church

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Shall happily make thee there a joyful bride.

speaker: JULIET
text: Now, by Saint Peter’s Church, and Peter too,

speaker: JULIET
text: He shall not make me there a joyful bride!

speaker: JULIET
text: I wonder at this haste, that I must wed

speaker: JULIET
text: Ere he that should be husband comes to woo.

speaker: JULIET
text: I pray you, tell my lord and father, madam,

speaker: JULIET
text: I will not marry yet, and when I do I swear

speaker: JULIET
text: It shall be Romeo, whom you know I hate,

speaker: JULIET
text: Rather than Paris. These are news indeed!

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Here comes your father. Tell him so yourself,

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: And see how he will take it at your hands.

speaker: CAPULET
text: When the sun sets, the earth doth drizzle dew,

speaker: CAPULET
text: But for the sunset of my brother’s son

speaker: CAPULET
text: It rains downright.

speaker: CAPULET
text: How now, a conduit, girl? What, still in tears?

speaker: CAPULET
text: Evermore show’ring? In one little body

speaker: CAPULET
text: Thou counterfeits a bark, a sea, a wind.

speaker: CAPULET
text: For still thy eyes, which I may call the sea,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Do ebb and flow with tears; the bark thy body is,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Sailing in this salt flood; the winds thy sighs,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Who, raging with thy tears and they with them,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Without a sudden calm, will overset

speaker: CAPULET
text: Thy tempest-tossèd body.—How now, wife?

speaker: CAPULET
text: Have you delivered to her our decree?

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Ay, sir, but she will none, she gives you thanks.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: I would the fool were married to her grave.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Soft, take me with you, take me with you, wife.

speaker: CAPULET
text: How, will she none? Doth she not give us thanks?

speaker: CAPULET
text: Is she not proud? Doth she not count her blessed,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Unworthy as she is, that we have wrought

speaker: CAPULET
text: So worthy a gentleman to be her bride?

speaker: JULIET
text: Not proud you have, but thankful that you have.

speaker: JULIET
text: Proud can I never be of what I hate,

speaker: JULIET
text: But thankful even for hate that is meant love.

speaker: CAPULET
text: How, how, how, how? Chopped logic? What is this?

speaker: CAPULET
text: “Proud,” and “I thank you,” and “I thank you not,”

speaker: CAPULET
text: And yet “not proud”? Mistress minion you,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Thank me no thankings, nor proud me no prouds,

speaker: CAPULET
text: But fettle your fine joints ’gainst Thursday next

speaker: CAPULET
text: To go with Paris to Saint Peter’s Church,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Or I will drag thee on a hurdle thither.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Out, you green-sickness carrion! Out, you baggage!

speaker: CAPULET
text: You tallow face!

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Fie, fie, what, are you mad?

speaker: JULIET
text: Good father, I beseech you on my knees,

speaker: JULIET
text: Hear me with patience but to speak a word.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Hang thee, young baggage, disobedient wretch!

speaker: CAPULET
text: I tell thee what: get thee to church o’ Thursday,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Or never after look me in the face.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Speak not; reply not; do not answer me.

speaker: CAPULET
text: My fingers itch.—Wife, we scarce thought us blessed

speaker: CAPULET
text: That God had lent us but this only child,

speaker: CAPULET
text: But now I see this one is one too much,

speaker: CAPULET
text: And that we have a curse in having her.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Out on her, hilding.

speaker: NURSE
text: God in heaven bless her!

speaker: NURSE
text: You are to blame, my lord, to rate her so.

speaker: CAPULET
text: And why, my Lady Wisdom? Hold your tongue.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Good Prudence, smatter with your gossips, go.

speaker: NURSE
text: I speak no treason.

speaker: CAPULET
text: O, God ’i’ g’ eden!

speaker: NURSE
text: May not one speak?

speaker: CAPULET
text: Peace, you mumbling fool!

speaker: CAPULET
text: Utter your gravity o’er a gossip’s bowl,

speaker: CAPULET
text: For here we need it not.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: You are too hot.

speaker: CAPULET
text: God’s bread, it makes me mad.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Day, night, hour, tide, time, work, play,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Alone, in company, still my care hath been

speaker: CAPULET
text: To have her matched. And having now provided

speaker: CAPULET
text: A gentleman of noble parentage,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Of fair demesnes, youthful, and nobly ligned,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Stuffed, as they say, with honorable parts,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Proportioned as one’s thought would wish a man—

speaker: CAPULET
text: And then to have a wretched puling fool,

speaker: CAPULET
text: A whining mammet, in her fortune’s tender,

speaker: CAPULET
text: To answer “I’ll not wed. I cannot love.

speaker: CAPULET
text: I am too young. I pray you, pardon me.”

speaker: CAPULET
text: But, an you will not wed, I’ll pardon you!

speaker: CAPULET
text: Graze where you will, you shall not house with me.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Look to ’t; think on ’t. I do not use to jest.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Thursday is near. Lay hand on heart; advise.

speaker: CAPULET
text: An you be mine, I’ll give you to my friend.

speaker: CAPULET
text: An you be not, hang, beg, starve, die in the streets,

speaker: CAPULET
text: For, by my soul, I’ll ne’er acknowledge thee,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Nor what is mine shall never do thee good.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Trust to ’t; bethink you. I’ll not be forsworn.

speaker: JULIET
text: Is there no pity sitting in the clouds

speaker: JULIET
text: That sees into the bottom of my grief?—

speaker: JULIET
text: O sweet my mother, cast me not away.

speaker: JULIET
text: Delay this marriage for a month, a week,

speaker: JULIET
text: Or, if you do not, make the bridal bed

speaker: JULIET
text: In that dim monument where Tybalt lies.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Talk not to me, for I’ll not speak a word.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Do as thou wilt, for I have done with thee.

speaker: JULIET
text: O God! O nurse, how shall this be prevented?

speaker: JULIET
text: My husband is on Earth, my faith in heaven.

speaker: JULIET
text: How shall that faith return again to Earth

speaker: JULIET
text: Unless that husband send it me from heaven

speaker: JULIET
text: By leaving Earth? Comfort me; counsel me.—

speaker: JULIET
text: Alack, alack, that heaven should practice stratagems

speaker: JULIET
text: Upon so soft a subject as myself.—

speaker: JULIET
text: What sayst thou? Hast thou not a word of joy?

speaker: JULIET
text: Some comfort, nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: Faith, here it is.

speaker: NURSE
text: Romeo is banished, and all the world to nothing

speaker: NURSE
text: That he dares ne’er come back to challenge you,

speaker: NURSE
text: Or, if he do, it needs must be by stealth.

speaker: NURSE
text: Then, since the case so stands as now it doth,

speaker: NURSE
text: I think it best you married with the County.

speaker: NURSE
text: O, he’s a lovely gentleman!

speaker: NURSE
text: Romeo’s a dishclout to him. An eagle, madam,

speaker: NURSE
text: Hath not so green, so quick, so fair an eye

speaker: NURSE
text: As Paris hath. Beshrew my very heart,

speaker: NURSE
text: I think you are happy in this second match,

speaker: NURSE
text: For it excels your first, or, if it did not,

speaker: NURSE
text: Your first is dead, or ’twere as good he were

speaker: NURSE
text: As living here and you no use of him.

speaker: JULIET
text: Speak’st thou from thy heart?

speaker: NURSE
text: And from my soul too, else beshrew them both.

speaker: JULIET
text: Amen.

speaker: NURSE
text: What?

speaker: JULIET
text: Well, thou hast comforted me marvelous much.

speaker: JULIET
text: Go in and tell my lady I am gone,

speaker: JULIET
text: Having displeased my father, to Lawrence’ cell

speaker: JULIET
text: To make confession and to be absolved.

speaker: NURSE
text: Marry, I will; and this is wisely done.

speaker: JULIET
text: Ancient damnation, O most wicked fiend!

speaker: JULIET
text: Is it more sin to wish me thus forsworn

speaker: JULIET
text: Or to dispraise my lord with that same tongue

speaker: JULIET
text: Which she hath praised him with above compare

speaker: JULIET
text: So many thousand times? Go, counselor.

speaker: JULIET
text: Thou and my bosom henceforth shall be twain.

speaker: JULIET
text: I’ll to the Friar to know his remedy.

speaker: JULIET
text: If all else fail, myself have power to die.
